column1: # TCGA Pan-Cancer (Xena) study name -> disease ontology id crosswalk.
# The Toil/Xena expression matrix (tcga_RSEM_gene_fpkm.gz) joins to
# long TCGA *study name* (e.g. "breast invasive carcinoma"), NOT the acronym. The
# Open Targets cttv `cancer2EFO_mappings.tsv` is keyed on the TCGA *acronym* and
# maps to EFO/MONDO ids that are mostly EFO synonym terms — only 4/33 of those ids
# are actually present in THIS graph's Disease set (the disease layer was imported
# from efo.json, which is MONDO-heavy). So a cttv-sourced mapping yields almost no
# This crosswalk therefore maps each of the 33 TCGA study names directly to the
# ontology id that EXISTS as a :Disease node in this graph (verified by name against
# the live graph, hand-curated — NOT runtime fuzzy-matched, which would silently
# mis-map). The `tcga_code` and `graph_node_name` columns record provenance. Rows
# whose closest term is a parent are flagged; 5 cohorts (ACC/MESO/PCPG/SARC/THYM)
# have no malignant node in the graph at all and are listed with their cttv id so
# the intent is documented — 13_tcga.py skips any ontology id absent from the graph.
# Reference data (same class as the cttv file it was derived from), NOT a tunable
# parameter. Coverage: 28/33 cohorts resolve to a present Disease node.

column4: head and neck squamous cell carcinoma
